narrative:
Springtime conditions continue around western North Carolina and Virginia to finish-out the weekend, although just not as warm as Saturday. There will be a few showers and perhaps an afternoon rumble of thunder. Sunny and dry weather returns for Monday as temperatures surge upward once again. The peak of the warmth will be Tuesday and Wednesday before showers Wednesday night lead to a colder Thursday, with even a few snow showers.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Sprung Forward

Last Updated Sun March 08 at 10:40AM by Scott Stephens

Springtime conditions continue around western North Carolina and Virginia to finish-out the weekend, although just not as warm as Saturday. There will be a few showers and perhaps an afternoon rumble of thunder. Sunny and dry weather returns for Monday as temperatures surge upward once again. The peak of the warmth will be Tuesday and Wednesday before showers Wednesday night lead to a colder Thursday, with even a few snow showers.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

Your forecast team is a bit groggy this morning after that extra-early ‘spring-forward’ start to the day. Today, we find a weakening cold front crossing the region that will continue a few showers and perhaps an afternoon thundershower. Thunderstorms won’t be as robust as what parts of the region experienced Saturday afternoon, however.

Dry air returns for Monday with abundant sunshine and more mild temperatures.

The springlike warmth culminates during Tuesday and Wednesday with some daily record high temperatures falling by the wayside. The weather during this period looks dry, save for an isolated afternoon shower or thundershower Tuesday afternoon.

A strong cold front is scheduled to arrive late Wednesday night. It will be pushing a line of showers and thunderstorms. Behind the front, a quick shot of colder air will change rain showers to snow showers early Thursday, ending during Thursday afternoon.

Under the influence of high pressure, dry weather can be expected for Friday and Saturday with a quick rebound in temperatures as Pacific air erases the Thursday cold shot.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for March 2, 2026

It's the first Monday Message for March! We discuss the chilly change for Monday and Tuesday, but the big weather story will be the spring fever that will be breaking out for the second half of the week.

Mar 02, 2026
Spring Fever and near-record temperatures

Warm weather is here, at least until the middle of next week. We will be flirting with daily records at a number of locations during this time period.

On Friday, high temperatures will be in record territory for many locations.

On this map, a '1' denotes a forecast high temperature that would surpass a high temperature record for the 6th of March.
